00:00 — The Stories Hitting Today
Quick setup of the three big threads: GPT-5.5 testing strong on UI, NSA using Mythos despite the blacklist, and Google forming a strike team under Sergey Brin.
01:56 — GPT-5.5 "Spud" Tests Are Leaking
OpenAI's shadow drop of GPT Pro updates, the front-end coding gains vs. Claude Opus 4.7, Polymarket pointing to an April 23 release, and Grok Build / Grok Computer entering the race.
04:42 — The Anthropic Effect: NSA Defies the Pentagon
How Claude's coding dominance is reshaping every lab, and why the NSA is using Mythos even after Anthropic got labeled a supply chain risk.
06:50 — Sergey Brin's Strike Team and the Flywheel
Why Brin personally came back to lead the coding push at Google DeepMind — and why coding agents are the first domino in the intelligence explosion.
09:22 — The Steve Yegge Drama: Is Google Really Behind on AI Adoption?
The viral tweet, the Demis Hassabis pushback, the two-tier Google vs. Google DeepMind divide, and Brin's reported "force every Gemini engineer" memo.
18:42 — Is Mythos Real, or Just Anthropic PR?
The skeptic camp vs. the pile of insiders — Jamie Dimon, the NSA, Powell, Bessent, Brin — who all appear to be taking it seriously.
